title: LONDON MOTH KILLER 10 (TEN) Premium Sticky Pheromone Moth Traps for the Common Clothes Moth and Similar Variants, for Wardrobes, Clothes and Carpets. inc. eBook (Discreet London Moth)
bullet_points: ✅ LONDON MOTH Sticky carpet and clothes pheromone moth traps are designed to monitor for, attract and restrain the most destructive house moths in the Europe, the Common Clothes Moth. Will also be useful to monitor for the Case Making and Pale Backed Moth. Each sticky Pheromone moth trap lasts for 4 months once opened and are non-toxic, odourless, and totally safe around inquisitive pets and children. Help to protect your wardrobe and other precious fabrics from existing and future generations of moth larvae.

✅ ADVANCED DISCREET DESIGN. The Common Clothes Moth is a negatively Phototactic species, meaning that they will actively avoid light and bright areas. Our dark interior coupled with the powerful pheromone laden glue in our moth traps will be doubly attractive to Common clothes moths compared to standard traps.

✅ The outer discreet and classy exterior will also not scream 'I HAVE MOTHS!' , ideal for those who would prefer to keep their moth problem under wraps and absolutely perfect for the hospitality industry.

✅ EASY SET UP. Honestly, it could not be easier. Remove the moth pheromone trap from the foil, peel the lining to reveal the glue, fold the trap into a triangle shape for hanging or flat placement and you are ready to go! No gross refills need to be changed. Once done with, pop the moth trap in the bin!

✅ CONTAINS 10 Discreet Common Clothes Moth pheromone traps. Also comes with our brand new moth busting e-Book 'THE DOMESTIC MOTH EXTERMINATOR'. The e-Book is chock full of professional tips to eliminate the scourge of moths from your home forever! Please follow the accompanying user guide to ensure best results from the clothes moths traps. Targets the Common Clothes Moth and can also be effective for the Case Making and Pale Backed Moth. NOT SUITABLE for The Brown House Moth, White Shouldered Moth, Indian Meal Moth and Bumble Bee Wax Moth.
